name: ClickHouse
description: Open-source columnar OLAP database designed for real-time analytics on large datasets, originally developed at Yandex

license.comment: Released as open source in 2016 under Apache 2.0 license

hosting-options.comment: ClickHouse Cloud is official managed service; third-party options include Altinity, Aiven, Instaclustr

initial-release.comment: Development started at Yandex in 2009, production use in 2012, open-sourced in June 2016

primary-language.comment: Built as a single, statically-linked binary without dependencies

data-model.comment: Columnar OLAP database with strong time-series capabilities; tables with rows and columns, schema-enforced

topology.comment: Replication is asynchronous and multi-leader; inserts can be issued to any node

sharding.comment: Uses hash-based sharding with configurable sharding key; sharding is optional and manually configured

replication-factor.comment: Each shard should have multiple replicas (three recommended) for high availability

multi-region.comment: Supports multi-datacenter deployments; tolerates failure of one datacenter with proper ZK/Keeper setup

cap-position.comment: Not consistent by default due to async replication; linearizability can be enabled. Prioritizes availability.

consistency-model.comment: Asynchronous replication means eventual consistency by default; can enable linearizable reads via select_sequential_consistency

acid-transactions.comment: No full ACID; experimental transaction support for MergeTree only. Single-statement operations are atomic.

transaction-scope.comment: INSERT into each partition is transactional on its own; no multi-statement transactions in production

isolation-levels.comment: MVCC provides Snapshot Isolation for reading queries

distributed-transactions.comment: INSERT into Distributed table is not transactional as a whole; each shard insertion is separate

query-language.comment: Full SQL dialect optimized for analytical queries

sql-compatibility.comment: Comprehensive SQL support including full JOIN support; optimized for OLAP workloads

secondary-indexes.comment: Supports various index types including sparse indexes; uses n-gram Bloom filter indexes for text

joins.comment: Full SQL Join support with 6 different join algorithms; GLOBAL JOIN for distributed scenarios

aggregations.comment: Highly optimized for aggregations; columnar architecture accelerates SUM, AVG, COUNT operations

full-text-search.comment: Full-text search now in beta; uses n-gram Bloom filter indexes

max-cluster-size.comment: ClickHouse prefers scale-up over scale-out; clusters with hundreds of nodes documented but specific max not stated

write-throughput.value: Billions of rows/day on single node
write-throughput.comment: Given enough resources, can process billions of records on a single server

read-latency-p99.comment: Columnar architecture enables sub-second queries across billions of observability events

auto-scaling.comment: ClickHouse Cloud supports automatic scaling with configurable limits

hot-spot-handling.comment: Sharding key selection is manual; automatic rebalancing not built-in

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Zero-downtime upgrades possible with rolling upgrade strategy across replicas

online-schema-changes.comment: Online ALTER TABLE stable since v20.3; some changes are metadata-only, others require part rewrites

point-in-time-recovery.comment: No native PITR; requires Kafka offset tracking for replay-based recovery

backup-strategy.comment: Supports full and incremental backups; clickhouse-backup tool for production use

kubernetes-operator.comment: Altinity Kubernetes operator available for automated ClickHouse cluster management

rust-driver.comment: Official pure Rust client: clickhouse-rs with serde support and TLS

go-driver.comment: Official Go driver: clickhouse-go with native protocol support

java-driver.comment: Official Java client and JDBC driver: clickhouse-java

python-driver.comment: Official Python driver: clickhouse-connect with Pandas and SQLAlchemy integration

ruby-driver.comment: Community drivers: click_house, clickhouse gem, clickhouse-activerecord

c-cpp-driver.comment: ClickHouse itself is C++; native protocol available for C/C++ integration

orm-support.comment: SQLAlchemy via clickhouse-connect; Entity Framework Core via EntityFrameworkCore.ClickHouse; ActiveRecord via clickhouse-activerecord

open-source.comment: True open source under Apache 2.0 license

free-tier.comment: ClickHouse Cloud offers a free trial tier

pricing-model.comment: Consumption-based pricing for compute, storage, and data transfer

cost-estimate-small.comment: Managed services have minimum monthly costs around $50-100 for smallest tiers

change-data-capture.comment: No built-in CDC for outbound streaming; ClickPipes supports inbound CDC from Postgres

watch-subscribe.comment: No native client subscription mechanism for data changes

triggers.comment: No database-side triggers; uses materialized views for data transformation

event-streaming.comment: Kafka table engine for consuming from Kafka; integrations with streaming platforms for inbound data
